# Backyard Bullies Wrestling Club

> Youth-to-adult wrestling club in Augusta, Georgia, serving the Central Savannah River Area (CSRA) since 2023. Programs for youth, middle school, high school, girls, and adult wrestlers in Folkstyle, Freestyle, and Greco-Roman. Your first 3 classes are free.

Backyard Bullies Wrestling Club was founded in 2023 by Coach Justin Boyden to build the wrestling community across the CSRA. The club trains beginners through national-level competitors and emphasizes hard work, discipline, and character on and off the mat.

## Key facts

- Location: 266 Bobby Jones Expressway (Rear Entrance), Augusta, GA 30907
- Phone: (706) 250-0186
- Email: backyardbullieswc@gmail.com
- Founded: 2023
- Styles: Folkstyle, Freestyle, Greco-Roman
- Ages served: pre-K and elementary age through middle school and high school, plus girls' and adult wrestlers (placement is by experience level)
- Offers: first 3 classes in any paid program are free; Sunday CSRA practice is always free for everyone; single drop-in is $15

## Programs & weekly schedule

Programs are by experience level and open to wrestlers of every age.

- Beginners Program — Monday, Wednesday, Friday at 5:00 PM
- Intermediate Program — Monday & Wednesday at 6:00 PM; Tuesday, Thursday & Friday at 5:00 PM
- Girls Program — Tuesday & Thursday at 6:00–7:15 PM
- Advanced Competition Team — Monday & Wednesday (Middle School & Beyond) at 7:15 PM; Tuesday & Thursday at 7:15 PM; Friday (Middle School & Beyond) at 6:30 PM
- Sunday — Free CSRA Practice, 2:00–4:00 PM (open to all wrestlers in the CSRA, always free)
- Private lessons available by booking on the club calendar

## Camps, clinics & events

- Homeschool Functional Fitness + Wrestling — a welcoming weekly program for homeschool athletes beginning August 5, 2026. Functional fitness with Coach Olivia meets Wednesdays for $50/month (4 sessions); add Friday wrestling with Coach Boyden for $90/month total (8 sessions). Details and registration: https://www.backyardbullies-wc.com/homeschool-functional-fitness/
- Summer Day Camp v4.0 — nine-week summer day camp (June & July, Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–3:00 PM) at the club in Augusta, GA; functional fitness, agility, speed, confidence, and leadership. Cost is a $25-per-week non-refundable deposit plus $100 per week due before the start date. Register: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfgSCAfWTCk7sJH0sRktNxaVqTXQHC6lr-feX9P1AE3P7sEjA/viewform
- Parents Night Out — safe, supervised event for children ages 3 (potty trained) through 8th grade, with Nerf War games and pizza on Saturday, July 18, 2026, 7:00–11:00 PM at the club. Registration is $30 for members and $40 for non-members. Details and registration: https://www.backyardbullies-wc.com/parents-night-out/
- JJ Howland Wrestling Clinic — one-day clinic with JJ Howland on Saturday, September 26, 2026, 10:00 AM–5:00 PM at the club; proceeds support Backyard Bullies WC athletes. Register: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScJxLVQ3eDVH3hcvaeQfWUXNmFb-UI7AvdkmkAEufFkQB0NgQ/viewform

## Membership & pricing

- Monthly membership: 2 practices/week $90, 3 practices/week $115, 4 practices/week $130, 5 practices/week $150
- Open mat / drop-in: $15 per session
- Discounts: U.S. Military Appreciation discount and Sibling discount (ask the club admin)
- Billing: invoice sent by email, or pay in person (call or text to enroll)

## Coaches

All Backyard Bullies coaches serve as volunteer coaches.

- Coach Justin Boyden — Founder & Head Coach. U.S. military veteran, two-time All-Army Wrestler, and Team Georgia and Team Colorado Wrestling national team staff.
- Coach Olivia Kendrick — Homeschool Program Head Coach. Homeschool mom of three with a competitive cheerleading background, focused on functional fitness, coordination, strength development, confidence, and healthy habits for homeschool athletes.
- Coach Hallee Milot — Strength Program Director. CrossFit Level 1 certified coach with over five years of training, competing, and lifting experience, focused on safe strength development, confidence, discipline, resilience, and mental toughness.
- Coach Katie Huitron — Assistant Coach. Lifelong mixed martial arts athlete with beach wrestling coaching experience, active training in MMA, kickboxing/Muay Thai, and wrestling, and a focus on confidence, discipline, resilience, and self-belief.
- Coach Ronnie Jones — Assistant Coach. Former Mercyhurst University wrestler and collegiate team captain at 184 pounds with a New York multi-sport background in wrestling, football, and lacrosse; focused on sound technique, discipline, confidence, and mental toughness.
- Coach Sean Houston — Lead Mat Coach. Augusta native, Harlem High School wrestler, and Brewton-Parker College competitor at 174 pounds with national-level, freestyle, and collegiate experience; focused on fundamentals, mat awareness, discipline, and competitive mindset.
- Coach Dale Schmacht — Folkstyle Program Director. 1979 Illinois state champion, three-time NCAA Division III All-American, three-time Hall of Fame inductee, and an academic trauma and critical care surgeon with an MD and a PhD in Physiology.
- Coach Katie Boyden — Co-Owner & Director of Administration and Beach Wrestling Coach. USA Wrestling pairing official with 15+ years in the sport, focused on club operations, events, and athlete development.
- Coach Dusty McKinney — Assistant Coach. George Mason University wrestler, All-Navy Wrestling Team All-American, American Ninja Warrior competitor, and 20-year U.S. Navy veteran focused on confidence, resilience, work ethic, and a love for wrestling.
- Coach Daniel Bange — Assistant Coach. Critical Care Registered Nurse and former two-time youth national champion (bird dog training) who coaches by example around discipline and consistency.
- Coach Rachel Bange — Assistant Coach. Former high school and Team Ohio wrestler and Registered Nurse (emergency medicine) who coaches alongside her husband; owner of Yankee Daisy Design Co.
- Coach Blaine Carter — Assistant Coach. 2002 Lakeside High School graduate, three-time region champion and state placer at 215 pounds, All-State football and wrestling athlete, and local burn center volunteer who helps young athletes build discipline, perseverance, accountability, and character.
